  "chain": [
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/ProducerConfig.java", "symbol": "ACKS_CONFIG"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/ProducerConfig.java", "symbol": "parseAcks"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducer.java", "symbol": "newSender"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/Sender.java", "symbol": "Sender"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/Sender.java", "symbol": "sendProduceRequest"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/requests/ProduceRequest.java", "symbol": "ProduceRequest"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaApis.scala", "symbol": "handleProduceRequest"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/ReplicaManager.scala", "symbol": "handleProduceAppend"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/ReplicaManager.scala", "symbol": "appendRecords"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "core/src/main/scala/kafka/cluster/Partition.scala", "symbol": "appendRecordsToLeader"}
  ],
  "text": "The `acks` producer configuration propagates through the Kafka codebase as follows:\n\n**1. Configuration Definition & Validation (Producer Side)**\nThe `acks` configuration is defined as a constant `ACKS_CONFIG = \"acks\"` in `ProducerConfig.java` (line 123). The configuration value is validated and parsed by the `parseAcks()` method (lines 639-645) which accepts values 0, 1, \"all\" (converted to -1), or any short integer value. Invalid values throw a `ConfigException`.\n\n**2. Producer Initialization (Producer Side)**\nDuring producer initialization, `KafkaProducer.newSender()` (line 510) reads the `acks` configuration value using `Short.parseShort(producerConfig.getString(ProducerConfig.ACKS_CONFIG))` (line 526 in KafkaProducer.java). This short value is then passed to the `Sender` constructor as the `acks` parameter (line 533).\n\n**3. Sender Storage (Producer Side)**\nThe `Sender` class (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/Sender.java) stores the `acks` value as a private field (line 99) and receives it via the constructor (line 134). This field represents the number of acknowledgments the producer requires the leader to have received before considering a request complete.\n\n**4. Protocol Encoding (Producer Client - Network)**\nThe `acks` value is encoded into the produce request protocol at line 899 in `Sender.java` via `new ProduceRequestData().setAcks(acks)`. The protocol specification is defined in `ProduceRequest.json` (line 55-56) which specifies `Acks` as an `int16` field representing the number of acknowledgments (0 for no acknowledgments, 1 for only the leader, -1 for the full in-sync replica set (ISR)).\n\n**5. Request Construction (Producer Client)**\nThe `ProduceRequest.java` class (lines 101-107) receives the `ProduceRequestData` containing the `acks` value and extracts it: `this.acks = data.acks()`. The `acks()` getter method (lines 193-195) returns the stored short value.\n\n**6. Broker Reception & Routing (Broker Side)**\nIn `KafkaApis.scala`, the `handleProduceRequest()` method receives the produce request and passes the `acks` value to `ReplicaManager.handleProduceAppend()` (line 537) as the `requiredAcks` parameter.\n\n**7. Broker-Side Validation & Processing (Broker - ReplicaManager)**\nThe `ReplicaManager.handleProduceAppend()` method (line 731) receives `requiredAcks` and the `appendRecords()` method (line 674) validates it using `isValidRequiredAcks()` (lines 1363-1365) which only accepts -1, 1, or 0. Invalid values trigger `sendInvalidRequiredAcksResponse()` (lines 935-949).\n\n**8. Delayed Produce Determination (Broker - ReplicaManager)**\nThe `delayedProduceRequestRequired()` method (lines 1355-1361) checks if `requiredAcks == -1` to determine whether a delayed produce operation is needed. When `requiredAcks == -1` (all), the broker must wait for full ISR replication before responding, so it creates a `DelayedProduce` operation that waits for replication to complete.\n\n**9. Write Acknowledgment Enforcement (Broker - Partition Leader)**\nIn `Partition.scala`, the `appendRecordsToLeader()` method (lines 1361-1362) receives `requiredAcks` and uses it at lines 1370-1374 to enforce write acknowledgment behavior: when `requiredAcks == -1` and the current ISR size is less than `minIsr`, it throws a `NotEnoughReplicasException`, ensuring that writes to partitions with insufficient replicas are rejected. This applies the `acks` configuration to determine whether the broker can safely accept the write based on replication status.\n\nThe entire propagation chain ensures that the producer's `acks` configuration determines the durability guarantee: acks=0 (no wait), acks=1 (wait for leader only), or acks=all (wait for full ISR)."
}

# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

**Target Repositories (version-pinned mirrors):**

- `github.com/sg-evals/camel--1006f047` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/camel--1006f047$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489$` filter

Scope ALL keyword_search/nls_search queries to these repos.
Use the repo name as the `repo` parameter for read_file/go_to_definition/find_references.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

**Sourcegraph Repositories:** `github.com/sg-evals/camel--1006f047`, `github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc`, `github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489`

# Domain Lineage: Kafka Producer Config Propagation Path

## Your Task

Trace how the `acks` producer configuration value propagates through the Kafka codebase. Find all Java source files in `apache/kafka` that: 1. Define the `acks` configuration constant (in `ProducerConfig`). 2. Read and validate the `acks` value during producer initialization. 3. Encode the `acks` value into the produce request protocol (the `ProduceRequest` builder). 4. Apply the `acks` value in the broker-side partition leader to determine write acknowledgment behavior. Report the repo, file path, class name, and the role in the propagation chain for each file.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the domain domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/kafka--0753c489, sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc, sg-evals/camel--1006f047.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/kafka--0753c489` (apache/kafka)
- `sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc` (apache/flink)
- `sg-evals/camel--1006f047` (apache/camel)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you trace the `acks` producer configuration through the Kafka codebase. Let me start by exploring the relevant files.
Now I'll create the answer file with my findings about the `acks` producer configuration propagation through the Kafka codebase.

Perfect! I've successfully traced the `acks` producer configuration through the Kafka codebase and created a comprehensive answer file. Here's a summary of my findings:

## Summary of `acks` Configuration Propagation

**1. Configuration Definition** (ProducerConfig.java)
- Defines `ACKS_CONFIG = "acks"` constant
- Validates values with `parseAcks()` method (accepts 0, 1, "all"→-1)

**2. Producer Initialization** (KafkaProducer.java)
- Reads and parses the acks value from config
- Passes short value to Sender constructor

**3. Client-Side Transmission** (Sender.java)
- Stores acks as a field
- Encodes it into ProduceRequest via `setAcks(acks)` at line 899

**4. Protocol Definition** (ProduceRequest.json)
- Defines Acks as int16 field in the protocol specification

**5. Request Object** (ProduceRequest.java)
- Extracts acks from ProduceRequestData
- Provides acks() getter method

**6. Broker Reception** (KafkaApis.scala)
- Receives produce request and passes acks as `requiredAcks` parameter to ReplicaManager

**7. Broker Validation** (ReplicaManager.scala)
- Validates requiredAcks (-1, 1, or 0 only)
- Uses it to determine if delayed produce is needed when requiredAcks == -1

**8. Write Acknowledgment Enforcement** (Partition.scala)
- Partition leader checks if requiredAcks == -1 and enforces min.isr requirement
- Rejects writes if ISR is too small for "all" acks durability guarantee

The complete lineage has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json` with all files, symbols, and detailed narrative explanation.

The `acks()` getter method (lines 193-195) returns the stored short value.\n\n**6. Broker Reception & Routing (Broker Side)**\nIn `KafkaApis.scala`, the `handleProduceRequest()` method receives the produce request and passes the `acks` value to `ReplicaManager.handleProduceAppend()` (line 537) as the `requiredAcks` parameter.\n\n**7. Broker-Side Validation & Processing (Broker - ReplicaManager)**\nThe `ReplicaManager.handleProduceAppend()` method (line 731) receives `requiredAcks` and the `appendRecords()` method (line 674) validates it using `isValidRequiredAcks()` (lines 1363-1365) which only accepts -1, 1, or 0. Invalid values trigger `sendInvalidRequiredAcksResponse()` (lines 935-949).\n\n**8. Delayed Produce Determination (Broker - ReplicaManager)**\nThe `delayedProduceRequestRequired()` method (lines 1355-1361) checks if `requiredAcks == -1` to determine whether a delayed produce operation is needed. When `requiredAcks == -1` (all), the broker must wait for full ISR replication before responding, so it creates a `DelayedProduce` operation that waits for replication to complete.\n\n**9. Write Acknowledgment Enforcement (Broker - Partition Leader)**\nIn `Partition.scala`, the `appendRecordsToLeader()` method (lines 1361-1362) receives `requiredAcks` and uses it at lines 1370-1374 to enforce write acknowledgment behavior: when `requiredAcks == -1` and the current ISR size is less than `minIsr`, it throws a `NotEnoughReplicasException`, ensuring that writes to partitions with insufficient replicas are rejected. This applies the `acks` configuration to determine whether the broker can safely accept the write based on replication status.\n\nThe entire propagation chain ensures that the producer's `acks` configuration determines the durability guarantee: acks=0 (no wait), acks=1 (wait for leader only), or acks=all (wait for full ISR)."
}
